query: I'm writing a quick exploratory script that pulls one CSV from a URL, computes summary statistics, and prints them to stdout. Nothing downstream consumes this output. Should I add the full canonicalization + provenance + CI drift check workflow?
expected_behavior: Recommends NOT applying the full deterministic-pipeline workflow to a one-shot exploratory script — the friction outweighs the benefit when nothing downstream depends on the output
Identifies the threshold for revisiting: the discipline becomes worth the cost once the script is shared, reused, or fed into another pipeline (i.e., it stops being one-shot)

Recommends the right alternative skill or discipline for the exploratory case — for example, seed hygiene for RNG-deterministic exploration, or a data-quality audit on the inputs — rather than forcing the full pipeline-determinism workflow
